name: Fort Polk
tagline: Home of the Joint Readiness Training Center, where light infantry units prepare for combat, in the Louisiana piney woods.
_note: Army combat-training post (Vernon Parish, west-central LA, near Leesville; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA LA115 Fort Polk; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). NAME HISTORY: Fort Polk -> Fort Johnson (June 2023) -> Fort Polk (2025; effective June 11, 2025, formal ceremony July 2025). Now honors Gen. James H. Polk (Silver Star; 4-star general), NOT the Confederate general Leonidas Polk (verified via KALB/NOLA/Army-press + Wikipedia, June 2026). Home of the Joint Readiness Training Center (JRTC). Medical: Bayne-Jones Army Community Hospital. Louisiana moved to TRICARE West in 2024. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 71446 (Leesville).

data.Location: Vernon Parish, LA (~10 mi east of Leesville, west-central Louisiana)
data.Role: Home of the Joint Readiness Training Center (JRTC)
data.Mission: Large-scale combat training rotations, focused on light infantry
data.Current name: Fort Polk (now honoring Gen. James H. Polk, Silver Star recipient)
data.Name note: Was Fort Johnson from 2023 to 2025 before reverting

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Fort Polk is home to the Joint Readiness Training Center, one of the Army's premier combat training centers, where brigades, especially light infantry, rotate through demanding, realistic exercises before deploying.

Set in the piney woods of west-central Louisiana near Leesville, the post carried the name Fort Johnson from 2023 to 2025 before reverting to Fort Polk, now honoring a Silver Star general rather than the Confederate general. It's a somewhat remote, training-focused community.

data.subtitle: Fort Polk is affordable and a bit isolated. The main choice is on-post housing versus the nearby town of Leesville, with the cost of living low either way.
intro: West-central Louisiana is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well, but the post is somewhat remote, so amenities are more limited than at a big-city base. On-post housing is popular; many also live in Leesville and nearby.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across Leesville and the surrounding Vernon Parish communities.

recommendations.advice: On-post housing keeps you close to work and is a popular choice given the remote location.
Leesville borders the area and is the main off-post community for housing, shopping, and schools.
The area is very affordable, so BAH tends to go a long way.
It's a somewhat isolated, training-driven post; lean into the outdoors and the community, with bigger cities a drive away.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Fort Polk site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Fort Polk has privatized on-post family housing across several neighborhoods, popular given the post's somewhat remote location.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA LA115 (Fort Polk)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Vernon Parish schools serve the post and the Leesville area.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Primary and specialty outpatient care with some inpatient services on post; complex care via regional network hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. At Fort Polk many families take on-post housing, but here's the framework if you're weighing Leesville.
rent: Your tour is short or your orders feel uncertain.
You prefer on-post housing for the short commute and community.
You'd rather not own in a small, somewhat isolated market.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent if you go off post.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The very affordable Leesville market fits your budget for a home you like.
You're comfortable with a small resale market and have found a place you trust.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. The area is inexpensive, so BAH stretches and buying can pencil out, but resale can be slow in a small, training-driven market, so many families take on-post housing. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics, and at a somewhat remote post, planning ahead helps.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-post lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: On-post housing is popular here; get on the list early and get the office's read on Leesville before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Louisiana vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact Vernon Parish schools early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Bayne-Jones Army Community Hospital or network providers.

data.q: Is it Fort Polk or Fort Johnson?
data.a: Fort Polk, as of 2025. It was Fort Johnson from 2023 to 2025; the name now honors General James H. Polk, a Silver Star recipient and four-star general, rather than the Confederate general Leonidas Polk.
It's home to the Joint Readiness Training Center (JRTC), where units, especially light infantry, run large combat-training rotations before deploying.
On-post housing is popular given the remote location; off post, Leesville is the main nearby community.
Fort Polk is MHA LA115. Use the calculator above for your grade.

picks.blurb: A huge lake on the Texas line known for fishing and boating.
